plan: 1. Create the OSINT card TypeScript interface in `src/ui/osint-card.ts` defining the `OsintCardData` type with fields: `title: string`, `value: string`, `source: string`, `confidence: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'`, `whyItMatters: string`. Follow the existing factory-function pattern used by `src/ui/receipt.ts`.

2. Implement the `createOsintCard(data: OsintCardData): HTMLElement` factory function in `src/ui/osint-card.ts`. Build DOM elements using `document.createElement`, compose the base `.card` class from `src/styles/card.css` with OSINT-specific child classes (`.osint-card-title`, `.osint-card-value`, `.osint-card-source`, `.osint-card-confidence`, `.osint-card-why`). Apply confidence-level modifier classes (e.g. `.osint-card-confidence--low`, `--medium`, `--high`) for badge styling.

3. Add OSINT card styles to `src/styles/osint-card.css`. Use existing design tokens (`--color-*`, `--spacing-*`, `--font-size-*`, `--radius-*`) for all values. Style the confidence badge using semantic colors (`--color-success` for low, `--color-warning` for medium, `--color-danger` for high). Ensure sufficient contrast, visible focus states, and `prefers-reduced-motion` support. Keep styling consistent with the financial-report / SOC visual direction.
4. Import `osint-card.css` in `src/styles/global.css` to include it in the build.

5. Implement a convenience function `createOsintCardList(cards: OsintCardData[]): HTMLElement` that renders multiple cards into a container div with class `.osint-card-list`, applying responsive grid layout (1 column mobile, 2 columns at `--breakpoint-md`).

6. Write unit tests in `tests/us-019/osint-card.test.ts` using the jsdom + Vitest pattern from the existing receipt tests. Test cases: (a) card renders with `.card` base class, (b) title text content matches input, (c) value text content matches input, (d) source text content matches input, (e) confidence badge renders with correct modifier class for each level, (f) 'why it matters' text content matches input, (g) `createOsintCardList` renders correct number of child cards.
7. Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify all checks pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test each acceptance criterion: verify title, value, source, confidence, and 'why it matters' are rendered as distinct DOM elements with correct text content
Test all three confidence levels (low, medium, high) produce the correct modifier CSS class on the badge element
Test that the card root element has the base `.card` class for shared styling
Test `createOsintCardList` renders the correct number of cards and wraps them in a `.osint-card-list` container
CSS file test: read `osint-card.css` and verify it references design tokens (no hardcoded colors or spacing values)
Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test` as gate checks before completion
risks: The existing `src/styles/card.css` base `.card` class is minimal — verify it provides enough foundation or extend it carefully without breaking the fingerprint receipt module that may also use it
Confidence level color mapping (success/warning/danger) must meet WCAG contrast requirements against the card surface background — verify token values
The component must remain purely presentational with no signal-collection logic mixed in, per the coding rules about separating data collection from rendering
